## reference · User Control and Freedom (Usability Heuristic #3)

* Users often make mistakes or change their minds; provide clear "emergency exits" to leave unwanted states without extended dialogues.
* Support undo and redo to allow users to backtrack on changes.
* Provide a **Back link** to return users to a previous page or screen.
* Provide a **Cancel link** to quit a task or multi-step process.
* Provide a **Close link** to close a new view.
* **Never disable the browser's Back button**; instead, offer valuable content to keep users engaged.
* When using overlays or lightboxes, ensure the **Back button has the same effect as a Close link**.
* Make **exit links easily discoverable** with clear labels and universal icons.
* Position **exit links in expected places**, such as the top right corner of an overlay.
* Allow users to **easily cancel an action** at any point with a clear and quick Cancel option.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/user-control-and-freedom/
